0: Fig.1: Some examples of an Agent using Tools
2: The implementation of tool use within the LangChain framework is a two-stage
3: process. Initially, one or more tools are defined, typically by encapsulating existing
4: Python functions or other runnable components. Subsequently, these tools are bound
5: to a language model, thereby granting the model the capability to generate a
6: structured tool-use request when it determines that an external function call is
7: required to fulfill a user's query.
8: The following implementation will demonstrate this principle by first defining a simple
9: function to simulate an information retrieval tool. Following this, an agent will be
10: constructed and configured to leverage this tool in response to user input. The
11: execution of this example requires the installation of the core LangChain libraries and
12: a model-specific provider package. Furthermore, proper authentication with the
